$comment: ADR-XXX (T9053) — Single source of truth for SQLite performance pragmas applied to every CLEO/SignalDock database connection. Consumed by: TypeScript via JSON import in packages/core/src/store/sqlite-pragmas.ts; Rust via build.rs include_str! + codegen in crates/signaldock-storage/build.rs producing SQLITE_PRAGMA_SQL. Drift is impossible by construction: both sides render the same `PRAGMA name = value` statements from this file. ADR review governs changes to the policy here.

policy.title: CLEO SQLite performance pragma policy
policy.description: Tuned for multi-process CLI workloads against shared project DBs (tasks.db, brain.db, conduit.db). WAL + NORMAL synchronous gives durable-on-commit with last-tx tolerance on power cut; busy_timeout queues concurrent writers; cache_size and mmap_size keep hot pages in memory.

sqliteVersionPin.rationale: RESOLVED (Saga T11242 substrate consolidation, supersedes T10281 hold). The live brain.db malformation on 2026-05-23 (errcode=11; `epic:T1075` signature) under node:sqlite v3.51.2 was the WAL-reset database-corruption class. SQLite 3.53.0 (2026-04-09, https://sqlite.org/releaselog/3_53_0.html) ships the upstream WAL-reset fix; 3.53.1 (2026-05-05) adds only regression point-fixes. Node 24.16.0 (2026-05-21) is the first Node release that bundles node:sqlite SQLite 3.53.0 — so the floor is raised from 24.13.0 to 24.16.0 to close the silent-corruption window (Node 24.13/24.14 ship the pre-fix 3.51.x). Driver decision (owner-ratified 2026-05-29): remain on first-party node:sqlite (DatabaseSync); do NOT adopt better-sqlite3 — 3.53.0 already carries the fix that matters and node:sqlite keeps the persistence layer zero-native-dependency. The pin stays soft (warn on drift, do not fail) — `packages/core/src/__tests__/sqlite-version-pin.test.ts` is the gate; the engines.node >=24.16.0 floor is the hard guard.

$fileInvariantsNote: T10310 — additional file-persistent invariants checked by `cleo doctor db-substrate` against every DB in DB_INVENTORY. NOT consumed by the Rust applicator (build.rs reads `pragmas` only). Format: [pragma_name, expected_value_as_string]. Values are compared case-insensitively. Expected page_size of 4096 matches the SQLite default; application_id 0 means no app-id tag (CLEO does not stamp one today).
$driftPragmasNote: T10310 — canonical PRAGMA queries that `cleo doctor db-substrate` walks for every DB in DB_INVENTORY. Drift is reported when the actual value (on a raw read-only snapshot — no pragma application) differs from the expected value declared in `pragmas` or `fileInvariants`.
